title: The World’s Fittest University! ($1 Billion Campus)

title: The World’s Fittest University! ($1 Billion Campus)

author: @Fernando-nm6kh

title: The World’s Fittest University! ($1 Billion Campus)
body:
As a wrestler, I feel like the video doesn't do justice for the sheer quality of the Penn State wrestling team.
Penn State has one of the best wrestling programss in the entire world. In the past decade, Penn State's wrestling program has produced some of the best wrestlers in the world. Several world and Olympic medallists have trained at Penn State. And it is breifly mentioned in the video that out of the 14 NCAA championships since 2011, Penn State has won 12 of them.
Also the wrestler he met in the video, Masanosuke Ono, probably had one of the most insane runs in recent wrestling history. In 2024, he beat Vito Arujau, the world champion from the year before, 12 - 0 as well as Zaur Uguev (an Olympic gold medallist and former world champion), 10 - 2. Dude seemed unstoppable for an entire year. He has unfortunately been out of competition recently due to injuries and other problems, but regardless, that man is insane.
author: @ISometimesEatCheese

title: The World’s Fittest University! ($1 Billion Campus)
body: The Penn State Wrestling Team is no joke.  Their coach is Cael Sanderson, considered the greatest college wrestler of all time (4x NCAA Champion with a 159-0 record) and a 2004 Olympian gold medalist.  Last year every wrestler in their lineup made the All Star Team (second time that has ever happened in NCAA history) and four won national championships in their respective weight classes.
author: @tcgtpl

title: The World’s Fittest University! ($1 Billion Campus)
body: Their largest and most successful program is not football, it is wrestling by far. They have the best wrestling team in the country, and the athletes in their team is world class. Their team is also undefeated for multiple years, and they have the best wrestling coaching staff in the country
author: @Urchebs

title: Columbia Student RANKS the Ivy Leagues...
body: To the high schoolers and young people that I imagine watch these videos types of videos, please do not put too much weight on a university’s ranking when making your decision. It’s nearly impossible to objectively rank a university and most of, if not all of the ranking methods are flawed in some way. In academia these rankings are NEVER considered and some of the best research is coming out of schools that a lot of students will write off due to their ranking.
author: @michaels8902

title: RESULTS OF COLLEGES I APPLIED TO AS A CURRENT IVY LEAGUE STUDENT #upenn #ivyleague #college
body: From my personal experience with the 2025 application cycle, after applying to 15 colleges and getting accepted by only 4, I'd say that quality matters more than quantity. I initially wished to get into many 'good' colleges as possible, but if I could go back in time, I would spend more time applying to colleges that truly fit me, regardless of prestige (although colleges do get better as prestige increases). And remember don't just apply to T10 schools, there are other schools that provide awesome college experience and are more affordable that are less heard of.
author: @LongNguyen-xs5do

title: RESULTS OF COLLEGES I APPLIED TO AS A CURRENT IVY LEAGUE STUDENT #upenn #ivyleague #college

title: RESULTS OF COLLEGES I APPLIED TO AS A CURRENT IVY LEAGUE STUDENT #upenn #ivyleague #college
body: I'm curious what you picked! I also applied to over 20 schools (Ivys and conservatories). I ended up attending Eastman and University of Rochester. It's the best of both worlds. Amazing education at both schools. As a UR only student you can still take lessons at Eastman or major in music at UR with all the same Eastman faculty. You can also apply to Eastman the following year and get a 5th year of aid. Comp Sci program is fantastic and most of the students I talk to all had incredible high school stats and went to UR after getting rejected from Ivy league. It's a great education here. In any case, best of luck making a decision!
author: @gabriellacariddo

title: PENN STATE PROS AND CONS #shorts #college #review #collegelife #school #schoollife
body:
As someone who lived in the area as a non-student, there is lots of great restaurants too! And a lot of them are only open when school is in session.

Con - State College, the town were Penn State is located can be quite stressful to navigate sometimes, especially when school is in. The streets are quite narrow and there can be A LOT of vehicular traffic. Also, don't be surprised if people walk out in front of you in the streets while you're trying drive. Evennid you're the one that has the right-of-way. It happens A LOT.

When students are in, it almost has a city feel to it. At least downtown does.
author: @TMIATC

body: The weather is so all over the place here too. In Georgia, we complain about it all the time, but it's on steroids here. body: Great video Kayla.   I graduated from Penn State 40 years ago, was an international student there.  What a ride it was !  My PSU friends at PSU are still my friends.  Went back a few times and just for football games.  Great school spirit. I will be going back this year and will take my brother from Argentina with me to the Ohio State game 🏈 I miss everything there, except winters 😃

title: pros and cons about PENN STATE (dorms, size, student life, etc)

author: @cdelarosaaa

title: pros and cons about PENN STATE (dorms, size, student life, etc)
body: Hi Kayla-----I was an out of state student like you and had to pay the extra tuition.  As I look back that extra money for tuition was the best money I ever spent.  I was at Penn State from 1963 to 1967 and I am 75 living in my condo in Clearwater Florida. Everything you described and said-------it was all true in 1963 when I set foot on campus at the Pollock dorms.  The tuition was $ 3,500 for out of state which might sound dirt cheap but that amount is like $ 20,000 now.  So every tuition dollar I spent paid me back 1,000 times AND IT WILL BE THE SAME FOR YOU.  Just wondering---are there a lot of sports cars there  now ??  It was flooded with them back then.  Anyways, keep up your videos they are excellent.  Regards, Tom Burke Class of 1967
author: @tomburke1687

title: pros and cons about PENN STATE (dorms, size, student life, etc)
body: Do you think you would be able to make a video about essentials for dorm life? Like what to bring? I’m a little worried about over or under packing. Clothing as well! I’m from NYC and Penn state is one of my top choices!
author: @mikaariku9151

title: Is an MBA Worth It? (IVY League WHARTON Business School EXPERIENCE)
body: A decade ago, my friend financed his prestigious Columbia MBA primarily through loans. Landing a high-paying consulting role at Boston Consulting Group (BCG), he strategically used his bonuses to rapidly eliminate that debt. This MBA wasn't just an education; it was the essential launchpad that propelled him to extraordinary success, transforming his career and securing his family's future. Without it, BCG's doors would have remained firmly closed
author: @ronalds271

title: Is an MBA Worth It? (IVY League WHARTON Business School EXPERIENCE)
body: Initially I thought it was worth it because it opened doors in a professional sense right out of school. A few years later it seemed like it was worth it because of the toolbox of skills one leaves school with. But in the long term, it seems like it was worth it because I usually had the belief that I could tackle any problem. Plus, where else but business school can you sit though an evening class while drinking a beer from a company reception and listening to Oprah giving weekly lessons on leadership?
author: @WrightViewLLC

title: Is an MBA Worth It? (IVY League WHARTON Business School EXPERIENCE)
body: I like how you said “if you’re more interested in the journey than the destination (it’s worth it) - well said.

title: Is an MBA Worth It? (IVY League WHARTON Business School EXPERIENCE)
body: from what I've seen the MBA is most worth it for people who kinda messed up during undergrad and then want a second chance at recruiting into traditionally high paying jobs like consulting, banking, tech PM, like I know a couple people who went from around 90k TC to 300k TC after their MBA because of the switch into a more lucrative field like IB

author: @thenbaplayer9485

title: Is an MBA Worth It? (IVY League WHARTON Business School EXPERIENCE)
body:
Hi Patrick, thanks for this video! I was genuinely impressed with the honesty that you put in your motivations as well as the pros and cons, and some of them really got me reflecting.
I'm actually at the stage in my career where I'm considering applying for MBA programs (mostly in the US for a perspective other than European where I come from), and I believe that we share some of the background - I'm not necessarily looking to break into any industry (most likely, I would be sponsored by a consulting firm), and by the time that I start the 1st year, I will have been married. I start having second thoughts if MBA really makes sense for me, as I'm not seeking to make hundreds of friends or learn business principles (having prior MSc in International Management). At this point I'm mostly oriented towards learning the American business culture, making business connections and possibly working towards being a business professor in the future - as you put it nicely, I'm more interested in the journey rather than the end. I'm wondering now - would you expect your return on this investment to be much more visible by the time you're 50 than it is now, to make it really worth it?
author: @Emylek10

title: Is an MBA Worth It? (IVY League WHARTON Business School EXPERIENCE)

title: Denzel Washington - University of Pennsylvania
body: Honestly, it breaks my heart how Manifest and Receive by Eva Hartley isn’t getting the attention it deserves. This book changed me. I saw the channel on youtube and my mind was blown. People need to wake up to this. Things have been flowing. money, peace, confidence. Maybe this is your moment too.

title: Denzel Washington - University of Pennsylvania
body:
Thank you for sharing this video, here are my speech notes:
• dreams without goals are just dreams
• life goals yearly goals daily goals
• discipline and consistency
• plan everyday work everyday
• hard work works
• Just because you are going a lot more doesn’t mean you are getting a lot more done.
• don’t confuse movement with progress
• it’s about what you do with what you have, not about what you have
• say thank you every morning in advance, for what is already yours
• each one, teach one
• aspire to make a difference
• nothing in life is worth it if you don’t take risk
• you will lose, you will embarrass yourself, you'll suck.
• to get something you never had, you have to do something you never did
• the world needs a lot and we need it from you
• what are you going to do with what you have?
• small minds discuss other people, good minds discuss ideas
• care for one another, love one another
• what an opportunity we have
Thank you For Reading My Notes! Because of this video I have made my own yearly-, monthly- and weekly-goals, which I share on my own channel, for people who need a reference.
author: @LucasPotjer

title: Denzel Washington - University of Pennsylvania
body: To all who's reading this, God bless you, and may your dreams come true, stay safe and have a wonderful day. ❤️

title: Denzel Washington - University of Pennsylvania
body: Thank you Denzel. My daughter was in a terrible accident, revived a tbi, her brain shifted, she broke her back in five places, tore her vagina and anus open when she was ejected, and multiple other injuries. She was not expected to make it. She was in a coma and life support. Needless to say, her recovery was unbearable for me, and the hardest fight she ever fought for her. We had to re teach her everything. I never let her give up. I never let her fall back. She fell seven times and got up eight. Four years later, she is soaring again. With God, with perseverance, and taking risks many people couldn't survive. There is so much more to what I call Sierra's journey, but what you said, is exactly what I did to get my child back. Thank you for taking time out for everyday people. I just wanted you to know how you affect people
author: @leeannesgambati8651

title: Denzel Washington - University of Pennsylvania
body:
“If I’m going to fall, I don’t wanna fall back on anything… I want to fall forward. At least that way I’ll see what I’m going to hit.”

title: If Cornell was honest #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague #cornell
body: I went to Cornell and made a ton of important connections and when I applied to grad school (medical school) a ton of my interviewers were Cornell alum and we were able to connect and just hit off immediately due to the connection.  I eventually went to NYU, where a solid 10% of my classmates were Cornell alum and had a fantastic time.  I’m now starting my academic career in medicine on the west coast, where, again I run into and hit it off with a gaggle of former Cornell grads and have instant connection.  Cornell changed my life, when I can, I will donate back to my Alma mater
author: @boy531

title: If Cornell was honest #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague #cornell
body: I spent a total of 16 years in Ithaca. 4 yrs undergrad, 8 yrs work, then 4 yrs at the vet school. It's a fine place with natural beauty. I never got bored or lacked for something fun to do. I don't know what this man's problem is.

title: If Cornell was honest #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague #cornell
body: I attend Cornell, and there are countless aspects of my school that I genuinely value, including numerous activities like sports, games, club events and so on. I never feel "stuck" inside the walls of Cornell - In fact, I only wish I could have so much time to explore everything that my school has to offer. I never experience any form of inferiority complex because of my association with this institution. You mentioned Andy Bernard and I must confess that I didn't know who he was until you brought him up, so any reference to him is quite irrelevant. It's essential to recognize that allowing one individual to shape your perception of an entire group is a flawed approach. Not everyone gets admitted into a competitive school because of a good SAT score. Instances of legacy admissions are present in various prestigious institutions, including Harvard and Yale, and drawing broad conclusions based on such factors is, in essence, an immature perspective. Let's focus on appreciating the unique qualities of each academic institution without letting isolated incidents overshadow the collective experiences of its diverse student body.
author: @moreblessingmushohwe6506
